qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection via user-controlled YAML in LifecycleGenerator
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and root cause: this finding concerns user-controlled YAML commands concatenated by LifecycleGenerator and executed by Jenkins sh(), while the advisory concerns SecurityIO.groovy using padLeft(32, '0') instead of padLeft(64, '0') for SHA-256 hex strings.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described SHA-256 hex string padding bug in SecurityIO.groovy. The only final qualifying finding is an unrelated command-injection report for YAML lifecycle generation.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-67rj-pjg6-pq59 identifies SecurityIO.groovy lines 622-626, where SHA-256 output is padded to 32 hex characters instead of 64.
command_injection_via_yaml/vulnerability_report.json identifies LifecycleGenerator.groovy YAML section concatenation and Jenkins sh() execution as the vulnerable path.
AUDIT_SUMMARY.md lists command_injection_via_yaml as the session's qualifying vulnerability folder; example/ and schema/ are reference artifacts, not final findings.
